title: NLP Basics Phase Complete
concept: PHASE B CAPSTONE. Concludes the NLP Basics phase. The boss exercise explain MUST end with the required handoff: 'Phase B: NLP Basics is complete. Next up: NLP Advanced.'

exercises.prompt: What do we call the branch of AI that helps computers understand human text and technology that helps computers read and understand human text?
exercises.explain: Natural Language Processing (NLP) is the field focused on enabling computers to understand and process human languages.

cards.title: Crossing the NLP Bridge
cards.body: You've learned how computers read! From breaking sentences into single words to cleanups and count lists, you are now ready to wrap up your basic NLP journey and prepare for the next step. Let's review what you've mastered!

cards.title: What is NLP?
cards.body: Natural Language Processing (NLP) is the technology that helps computers read, understand, and process human languages. By breaking text down into smaller tokens and cleaning up unnecessary words, we prepare human language for computer models to analyze.

cards.title: The Translation Guide
cards.body: Think of basic NLP like building a translation guide between humans and computers. Just like a dictionary helps you translate word-for-word, basic NLP uses tools like tokenizers and vocabulary lists to help computers read our words before they try to understand their deeper, secret meanings.

cards.title: NLP in Action
cards.body: When you type a search query like 'best pizza near me!', a basic NLP system will clean it up by removing the exclamation mark, splitting it into ['best', 'pizza', 'near', 'me'], and looking up those words in its database.

cards.title: Do Computers Really 'Read'?
cards.body: People often think that NLP models instantly understand human feelings and context like a real person. In reality, basic NLP models just see words as math and numbers. They look for patterns in the numbers, not 'meaning' in the way humans do!

cards.title: Text vs. Data
cards.body: See how raw human text gets transformed into structured data that computer models can actually calculate.

left.text: 'Hello, World!' - A messy string containing capital letters and punctuation marks.

right.text: ['hello', 'world'] - Lowercase words separated and stripped of punctuation.

exercises.prompt: Why do we clean up text by removing extra punctuation and common filler words in basic NLP?

options.text: To make the sentence longer and more complex
To translate the text into another language instantly
To clean the text so the computer only analyzes the important words
To change the font size of the text

exercises.explain: Removing punctuation and common filler words (like 'the' or 'is') helps the computer focus on the most important words in the text.

exercises.prompt: True or False: NLP systems must convert text into numbers before a computer can perform calculations on it.
exercises.explain: Computers cannot understand raw words directly. They must convert text into numbers (vectors) to process and analyze them.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how NLP models split up sentences.
exercises.sentence: To read a sentence, an NLP system first splits the text into small pieces called tokens.
exercises.explain: Tokenization is the process of breaking a sentence down into individual units called tokens, such as words or punctuation marks.

exercises.prompt: Which of these models is performing a basic NLP task?

options.text: Model A: 'I scanned the image and detected a brown dog near a tree.'
Model B: 'I split the sentence into 5 tokens and removed the exclamation point.'
Model C: 'I calculated the joint angles to move the mechanical arm forward.'

exercises.explain: Model B shows an AI analyzing human text structure (tokens and vocabulary), which is the core of Natural Language Processing. Model A is doing computer vision, and Model C is doing robotics.

exercises.prompt: Now that you have completed this phase, which of these best summarizes what we learned to do in NLP Basics?

options.text: Training computers to write complete, original fantasy novels from scratch
Breaking text into tokens, cleaning it, and converting it to numbers
Building physical robots that can speak with human voices
Designing computer hardware chips specifically for computer vision

exercises.explain: Exactly! Phase B: NLP Basics is complete. Next up: NLP Advanced.
